title: Commercial Graduate Sales Path - Swedish Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Sales **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **FY27 Grad Program Job Description \- Sales Path** **Fixed\-term contract** **Duration:** 12 months **Start Date:** August 2026 We are thrilled to invite ambitious graduate candidates who would like to build a career in **Salesforce** to apply for the **5th cohort** of our **EMEA Graduate Rotation Program – Sales Path** . This exciting **12\-month program** is designed to give graduates exposure to both sales and solution engineering. Having built the foundational skills for both roles and met our eligibility criteria, top graduates on this path can begin their **Salesforce Sales** careers. The promotion window opens from **month 7 onwards.** Our graduate program is a fully immersive experience, combining comprehensive learning paths with in\-role experience. You will be assigned experienced **Sales Development Buddys** who will provide hands\-on coaching, invite you to join their discovery and qualification calls, giving you the confidence to pick up the phone to reach out to your first salesforce customers. You will also be paired with experienced **Solution Engineer Buddies** during the SE Rotation who will involve you in their live deals. Throughout the programme, you will gain exposure to senior sales and solution engineering professionals during our inspiration sessions. By joining Salesforce, you will have the opportunity to lay the foundation for a **fast\-paced, successful career** . We are a proud member of the **Fortune 100 Best Places to Work** list for the past 14 years and a consistent winner of **Great Place to Work** awards. Who are we looking for? * You are a 2025 or 2026 graduate (minimum bachelor’s degree) from all academic backgrounds * You are interested in the impact technology can have on the world and business * You are a creative problem\-solver * You are a relationship builder and possess a growth mindset * You are curious, self\-motivated, and focused on continuous learning * You thrive in a team environment * You have excellent interpersonal skills * You are goal\-oriented and comfortable in a fast\-paced environment * You are fluent in English \& Swedish What does our EMEA Graduate Program look like? **The Program Is Structured As Follows** Introduction Month During your first month, you will receive extensive onboarding enablement through a Salesforce\-style academy, where you will: * Become immersed in the culture of Salesforce and gain a deep understanding of our values * Develop an understanding of our solutions, technologies, and tools * Learn critical professional skills for your career at Salesforce * Follow a structured learning calendar with sessions and hands\-on workshops led by experts and program alumni Rotations During the Graduate Program, graduates on both the Sales and Solution Engineering Paths will follow the same curriculum and complete the same rotations. Essentially you will gain exposure to both the **Sales and Solution Engineering Roles** . The structure of each rotation is similar: you will spend **60% of your time** immersed in the role with clear tasks, targets, and stakeholder alignment, while the remaining **40%** will focus on role\-specific learning. You will be paired with experienced buddies in each path. If your first rotation (months 2 through 4\) is in **Sales** , you will then join the **Solution Engineering Rotation** from months 5 through 7, and vice versa. Over these six months, you will have built the fundamental skills for these complementary disciplines. Having gone deeper on your solution knowledge, learning how to demo and having exposure to later stages of the sales cycle during the solution engineering rotation will set you up for sucess as you promote throught the various sales roles \- SDR, BDR, AE and beyond. Throughout the rotations, there will be **self\-paced certifications and skills assessments** . You must meet all eligibility criteria before you can **panel for promotion** . Examples of what you can expect during the rotations **Sales Graduate Rotation** * Learn Salesforce’s best\-in\-class prospecting and qualification methodologies * Learn how to use social, digital, video, chat, and phone platforms to effectively engage Salesforce prospects * Collaborate with Sales Development Specialists to run campaigns and help build sales pipeline for the EMEA Sales organisation Solution Engineering Graduate Rotation * Learn our solutions at a deeper, more technical level * Work closely with the Solution Engineering organisation, supporting the creation of cutting\-edge customer demonstrations * Learn the art of customer discovery and how to create a compelling customer point of view What’s in it for you? * A clear and structured career path within our Sales organisation * The opportunity to work in an international team within a nurturing environment * Access to our diverse internal employee groups * A comprehensive benefits package * Relocation budget and guidance throughout your relocation process * An additional seven days of paid time off for charitable causes – learn more about our 1\-1\-1 model Map your future for the coming years With the **EMEA Graduate Program – Sales Path** , you can continue to progress: * After your Grad Program Rotations you can progress to Sales Development Representative, Business Development Representative and then Account Executive, where you will position our solutions to potential customers. In some instances, this path may vary or be subject to change. * With our strong mentoring culture, we strive to develop industry\-leading Solution Engineering leaders. This is a phenomenal opportunity to build international experience within an organisation that has transformed the enterprise software market through cloud computing. Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: Community Operations Analyst - Estonian
description: We are looking for fluent Estonian speakers to join Community Operations team with a large social media in Dublin. This multicultural and diverse team supports social media communities across the world, helping them to create better and safer online environment for all users. * **No previous experience required – full training is provided!** Your main tasks will be investigating and resolving reported content, by following company’s policies and local legal regulations. These reports might include different types of content, including pictures, videos, and posts, where some of them can be of a sensitive nature. Due to that, strong personal resilience is a must do in this role. This is an excellent opportunity to work in professional multicultural team, to gain valuable transferable skills, and build a great network of friends and professionals in a vibrant business culture. Main Responsibilities * Investigating and resolving reported issues (via ticketing system), such as requests for account support or potentially abusive content * Enforcing clients Terms of Use * Using market knowledge and insights to find solutions to improve the support of client’s community of users * Gathering, analysing, and utilising data to develop ways to improve the overall user experience * Recognising trends and patterns and escalating issues to the global team * Identifying inefficiencies in workflows and suggest solutions Skills \& Competences * Strong personal resilience with the ability to operate in a pressured and fast paced environment * Strong interpersonal skills, verbal, and written communication skills and most importantly – empathy * High adaptability and positive approach to challenges * Ability to make decisions, prioritise, solve problems and work as part of a team * Effective time management \& prioritisation skills **Language Skills:** * Fluent in **Estonian** on the near\-native level in oral and written communication, with in depth understanding of culture and social trends in the region * **Advanced level of English** * Please note, both languages will be tested **Benefits and perks:** * Internal career progression opportunities * Fully subsidised healthcare * 25 days of annual leave * Employee assistance programme (EAP) * PRSA Scheme * Extensive Training \& Development * Annual company get\-togethers and employee engagement events * Bike to work scheme * Free parking on\-site Location: Sandyford (D18\), this is an office job Salary: €31,063 p/y Performance Bonus: up to 10% Working hours: 37 per week, occasionally weekends and bank holidays Shift: **Day** shift 9am\-5\.30pm or **Evening** shift 6pm\-2am. For the Evening shift: 25% salary uplift for the late hours from 10pm to 2am and €12 per day (before tax) as travel allowance. No shift rotations – fixed working schedule. Pay Schedule: Monthly Contract Type: Permanent \#CovalenLinkedIn \#LI\-JS5

title: Consultant - Food & Agribusiness
description: **About Farrelly Mitchell** Farrelly Mitchell helps the world’s most ambitious food and agribusiness companies make the right decisions. With expert insight and local market intelligence, we build and implement sustainable strategies that help clients restructure, transform, and grow. From offices across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, we serve corporates, sovereign wealth funds, private equity and institutional investors, development finance institutions, governments, and multilateral agencies, delivering over $20 billion in due diligence and transaction advisory mandates alongside a deep portfolio of food systems and development engagements. **Our Consulting Team** Our Consulting Team supports clients across the firm’s three service pillars: commercial and technical advisory; food systems advisory; and development and impact advisory. The team handles the firm’s most complex and commercially significant engagements across our regional markets, supported by an experienced network of subject matter experts. **Purpose of the role** In response to growing demand for our services, we are seeking an exceptional Consultant to join the Consulting Team. The successful candidate will bring proven experience from a Big 4 or top\-tier consulting firm, paired with a strong understanding of the food and agribusiness sectors. Reporting to the Head of Europe, the Consultant will own the end\-to\-end delivery of high\-quality client engagements, including commercial and technical due diligence, build trusted relationships with senior client stakeholders, and mentor junior consultants. Based at our global headquarters in Balbriggan, Co. Dublin, the role involves frequent international travel across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. **Key responsibilities** *Client engagement \& delivery* · Lead the end\-to\-end delivery of client engagements, ensuring high\-quality outputs on scope, on budget, and on time. · Lead commercial and technical due diligence, assessing assets, operations, and markets, and translating findings into clear, decision\-ready conclusions. · Manage day\-to\-day client relationships, becoming a trusted adviser to senior stakeholders. · Apply rigorous analytical frameworks and structured problem\-solving to complex agribusiness questions across diverse geographies and sub\-sectors. · Manage project teams and subject matter experts to meet or exceed client expectations. *Sector expertise \& advisory* · Advise clients across the firm’s full service range: commercial and technical advisory (due diligence, market intelligence, feasibility and financial modelling, risk advisory, strategy and execution, and value creation); food systems advisory (sustainability and ESG, climate and natural capital, food safety and traceability, agtech and foodtech, and policy and regulatory compliance); and development and impact engagements with governments, development finance institutions, and multilateral agencies. · Synthesise commercial, technical, and market insight into clear recommendations that inform high\-stakes client decisions. · Deliver food systems engagements, including ESG assessments, supply chain and traceability reviews, food safety and policy advisory, and food security and development programmes for sovereign, DFI, and multilateral clients. · Maintain a current view of sector developments, market trends, and regulatory shifts across the firm’s target geographies. *Business development \& growth* · Contribute to proposals, pitches, tenders, scope, methodology, team, and fees. · Identify follow\-on opportunities within existing client relationships and support new business development. · Contribute to thought leadership and marketing, including articles, webinars, and social media content. *Team leadership \& development* · Lead engagement teams and workstreams, providing direction, quality assurance, and support. · Mentor and develop junior consultants, building capability within the team. · Contribute to recruitment, onboarding, and continuous improvement of team processes. **What does success look like?** · Success is measured by client outcomes, delivery quality, and your growing contribution to the firm. In your first year, a strong Consultant would: · Lead multiple client engagements end\-to\-end, on scope, on budget, and to a standard that earns repeat business. · Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders across our markets in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. · Become a go\-to expert on one or more food and agribusiness sub\-sectors or service lines. · Contribute materially to winning new business, including follow\-on work and strong proposals. · Elevate the performance and confidence of junior consultants through hands\-on mentoring. **Essential criteria** · Proven consulting experience at a Big 4 or top\-tier firm, with a strong delivery track record. · Strong understanding of the food and agribusiness sectors and the commercial, technical, and market dynamics that shape clients’ decisions across the value chain. · Track record of leading complex engagements across due diligence, market intelligence, feasibility, or strategy. · Excellent analytical and structured problem\-solving skills. · Outstanding written and verbal communication, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders. · A master’s degree in a relevant discipline (business, economics, agribusiness, food systems, or finance). · Willingness to travel frequently across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. **Desirable criteria** · An MBA or other relevant postgraduate qualification. · Direct experience working in or with clients in our priority EMEA markets. · Prior experience in agribusiness investments, M\&A due diligence, or value\-creation engagements. · Familiarity with development finance institutions, multilateral programmes, or sovereign clients. · Experience on food systems engagements, ESG, traceability, agtech, supply chain, or policy advisory. · Working knowledge of a second language relevant to our markets (e.g., French, Arabic, Spanish). **Personal attributes** · Strategically minded and commercially astute, with sharp judgement on client and project priorities. · Excellent project\-management skills and the composure to manage multiple engagements simultaneously. · Collaborative and influential, able to bring out the best in colleagues, partners, and SMEs. · Self\-directed, calm under pressure, and unwavering in the pursuit of exceptional client outcomes. **What we offer** · A dynamic role in a growing international firm in the global food and agribusiness sector. · Exposure to high\-profile clients and complex engagements across Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. · A competitive salary package and a long\-term incentive scheme for the ideal candidate. · A clear path to further progression within the firm. · A collaborative, expert\-led culture and the support of a global network of sector specialists.

title: Content Creator – FinTech, Dublin Shoot
description: This role is ideal for a creative professional with a passion for producing engaging short\-form video content in the FinTech sector. The position involves a one\-day, on\-site shoot at a Dublin studio, where you will be responsible for recording and editing a suite of videos designed to fuel a month’s worth of social media activity. The focus is on founder\-led and user\-generated content (UGC) that communicates the innovative nature of a fully digital pension product. You will collaborate closely with the founding team to capture authentic, high\-impact footage that aligns with the brand’s vision and messaging. The project must be completed by the end of June, and only candidates based in Dublin will be considered. **Deliverables** * Record and edit a series of short\-form videos during a one\-day studio shoot in Dublin * Produce content suitable for various social media platforms, ensuring a month’s worth of posts * Capture founder\-led and UGC\-style footage that highlights the digital pension product’s unique value * Edit and deliver polished, ready\-to\-publish video assets in line with provided style references * Collaborate with the team to ensure content aligns with brand guidelines and campaign objectives **Requirements** * Proven experience in content creation, video editing, and short\-form video production * Strong portfolio demonstrating UGC and founder\-focused content, ideally for startups or FinTech brands * Proficiency with video editing software (e.g., Adobe Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or similar) * Ability to work efficiently during a single\-day shoot and deliver high\-quality edits within tight deadlines * Excellent communication and organizational skills * Must be based in Dublin and available for on\-site work; applicants from outside Dublin will not be considered * Familiarity with social media trends and best practices for video content **About Twine** Twine is a leading freelance marketplace connecting top freelancers, consultants, and contractors with companies needing creative and tech expertise. Trusted by Fortune 500 companies and innovative startups alike, Twine enables companies to scale their teams globally. **Our Mission** Twine's mission is to empower creators and businesses to thrive in an AI\-driven, freelance\-first world.

title: Customer Assistant (Permanent 25 - 30 Hours) - Drumcondra
description: **About the role** We pride ourselves on offering customers the biggest range of products around. And that's not just food, but everything from clothes and kettles to financial services. But did you know we offer the biggest job opportunities too? With stores across the UK and Ireland, and a variety of roles, we are always looking for people who are as passionate about helping customers as we are. Our Every Little Helps approach doesn't just apply to our customers. It's at the very heart of everything we do at work â€“ and it's what makes us different. The service is also available to families of colleagues who live at the same address for a reduced cost. * 24/7 access to our Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for you and family members for in the moment emotional support along with free access to a range of wellbeing services supporting you under our new Wellbeing First approach of Body, Mind and Life. * Colleague Clubcard offering 10% discount across stores and online, with 15% discount weekends once a month (Friday to Sunday), and 20% off twice a year. Â Our core purpose is â€œServing our customers, communities and planet a little better every dayâ€. Â Serving means more than a transactional relationship with our customers. Â It means acting as a responsible and sustainable business for all stakeholders, for the communities we are part of, and for the planet. Through initiatives like the Tesco Community Fund, our association with Childrenâ€™s Health Foundation, our partnership with FoodCloud and our Stronger Starts programme, we have supported over 21,000 community groups, raised over â‚¬7\.5 million for medical equipment, and provided almost 15 million meals nationwide, to those living in food insecurity. Tesco has operated in the Irish retail market since 1997, and with 170 stores nationwide, we employ over 13,000 people in cities and towns around Ireland, supporting almost 45,000 jobs directly and indirectly. Tesco is the single largest buyer of Irish food and drink in the world, buying â‚¬1\.6 Billion a year; more than any other single country in the European Union, more than even the USA which you perceive as a massive buyer of Irish food. Â We work closely with suppliers across Ireland who are dedicated to making the best products for our customers and this is reflected in the numerous awards our products have won over the years including BlÃ¡s Na hÃ‰ireann, World Steak Challenge and Great Taste Awards. We are proud to have an inclusive culture at Tesco where everyone truly feels able to be themselves. It is embedded in our values: we treat people how they want to be treated. Â At Tesco, we not only celebrate diversity, but recognise the value and opportunity it brings. Â We always want our colleagues to feel they can be themselves at work and we are committed to helping them be at their best. We know life looks a little different for each of us. So, talk to us throughout your application about how we can support. Tesco is committed to celebrating diversity and everyone is welcome at Tesco. We're committed to providing a fully inclusive recruitment process, allowing candidates the opportunity to thrive and inform us of any reasonable adjustments they may require. For further information on the accessibility support we can offer, please click http://roi.tesco\-careers.com/accessibility/ **How can I let Tesco know I need additional support?** Please let us know you need additional support by emailing **tescoireland.recruitment@tesco.ie** This will allow us to understand more about you and how we can best support you through the recruitment process.

title: Customer Service Graduate
description: **Description** Do you want to join the best Graduate Programme in Aviation? Joining the the Ryanair Graduate Programme not only means joining Europe’s Largest Airline Group but will give you the opportunity to learn from some of the most commercially astute, financially minded, and creative minds in the airline industry and you will forge relationships for life. Our unique programmes offer you to chance to rotate through a number of key business areas in your chosen programme, will get continued mentorship from our senior leadership team, be brought on a number of trips around the network to better understand Ryanair and it’s operations and some well\-desevered nights out **The Programme** Within the Customer Service Graduate Programme, you will get to do 4 rotations within the following areas:\- Legal, Operations, Digital development and an overseas placement with our claims centre in Madrid. **Operations** * Contact centre Vendor Management * Customer Journey Mapping and process improvement initiatives * Voice of the customer programme and CSAT performance improvement **Legal** * EU261 Passenger rights legislation. * Working with our finance partner on managing down the cost of claims **Digital Development** * Working with the Digital team to define the self\-service functionality that makes life easier for customers * Automating workflow and driving efficient processes. * Business Analysis, Business case creation and exposure to building functional requirements specification. **Overseas Operation in Madrid** * You will have the opportunity to spend a period of time with our team in Madrid understanding the role of the legal team in managing claims and costs for the business **The Application Process** **What you need to get in –** You will need to be on track to achieve a minimum of 2\.1 and have excelled in your degree course. We want inspirational graduates who have a story to tell and who have strong outside interests. Cultural fit at Ryanair is just as important as academics. **Application process –** Application will remain open until **31st January 2025** But we recommend getting your application in earlier as like our aircraft we like to be on\-time! Everyone we hire at Ryanair needs to show the skills and behaviours that have helped us to achieve our growth over the last 40\+ years and we expect candidates to demonstrate a hardworking and entrepreneurial spirit during the interview process, which will include a mandatory face to face interview. **Application form** – To ensure you are putting your best foot forward, please make sure that you pay attention to the application and ensure that all information supplied is accurate. In the cover letter please state why you want to join the Ryanair Graduate Programme and let us know about which areas that you are particularly interested in working in. **Requirements** * Studies in a relevant degree programme and either secured a 2\.1 or above or be on track to * Must of graduated/ finished university no earlier than 2023 * Hold the unrestricted right to live and work in Ireland and travel freely throughout the network. * Strong analytical ability to drive efficiency, quality and continuous improvements * Ability to work in a pressurised and fast paced environment * Strong organisational and interpersonal skills * The ability to work on own initiative * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * PC Skills (Word, Excel, Power point etc) * Good sense of humour and doesn’t take themselves too seriously!!!! **Benefits** * Competitive salary that increases in Year 2! * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 250 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme – Matched up to 5% or €5,000 * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies Interpersonal Skills Computer Skills Organisational Savvy

title: Customer Service Specialist Part Time

Training \& Onboarding Successful candidates will complete a full\-time structured training and onboarding programme before moving onto the flexible part\-time rota. Training will include: * Classroom learning * Live system walkthroughs * Shadowing and reverse shadowing * Call handling support * Practical troubleshooting exercises * Ongoing coaching and development FOODHUB IS GROWING — AND WE’RE BUILDING A CUSTOMER SERVICE CENTRE OF EXCELLENCE Foodhub is continuing to expand its Dublin operations team and we are looking for experienced customer service professionals to join our Frontline Services department. Our Frontline Services team plays a critical role in keeping businesses operational and supported during busy trading periods. to resolution * Troubleshoot operational and technical issues * Communicate professionally under pressure * Deliver high\-quality support during peak operational periods * Escalate issues resolution. **Resolve** Focus on solutions and positive outcomes. **Educate** Help clients better understand the tools and systems they use. **What We Offer** * Flexible rotational weekend scheduling * Structured training and onboarding programme * Career progression opportunities * High\-performance operational environment * Supportive and collaborative team culture * Modern Dublin office environment Additional Information This role is best suited to individuals who: * Enjoy fast\-paced operational environments * Are comfortable working during peak business periods * Take pride in delivering strong customer experiences * Want to develop within a structured and growing technology company If you enjoy problem\-solving, working as part of a high\-performing team, and supporting businesses during critical trading periods, we would love to hear from you. Pay: €17\.00 per hour Application question(s): * This role is fully on\-site at Dublin Landings, Dublin 1 (D01 V4A3\). Are you able to reliably commute to this location for every shift? * This role requires rotational weekend shifts between Friday and Monday. Are you available to work this schedule on an ongoing basis? * Do you have previous experience handling customer enquiries over the phone in a call centre environment? Work Location: In person

title: Customer Support Engineer - Ireland
description: **Who is Heidi?** Heidi is building an AI Care Partner that supports clinicians every step of the way, from documentation to delivery of care. We exist to double healthcare’s capacity while keeping care deeply human. In 18 months, Heidi has returned more than 18 million hours to clinicians and supported over 73 million patient visits. Today, more than two million patient visits each week are powered by Heidi across 116 countries and over 110 languages. Founded by clinicians, Heidi brings together clinicians, engineers, designers, scientists, creatives, and mathematicians, working with a shared purpose: to strengthen the human connection at the heart of healthcare. Backed by nearly $100 million in total funding, Heidi is expanding across the USA, UK, Canada, and Europe, partnering with major health systems including the NHS, Beth Israel Lahey Health, MaineGeneral, and Monash Health, among others. We move quickly where it matters and stay grounded in what’s proven, shaping healthcare’s next era. Ready for the challenge? **About Your Role** As a **Support Engineer at Heidi Health** , you’ll apply deep technical expertise to diagnose, resolve, and prevent complex issues in our AI\-powered healthcare product. Your work directly impacts product reliability, customer trust, and Heidi’s ability to scale safely in high\-stakes clinical environments. By owning technical issues end\-to\-end and partnering closely with engineering and product teams, you’ll play a critical role in supporting clinicians, improving patient care, and contributing to the long\-term success of our company. **What You’ll Do:** * Act as a direct point of contact for customers on complex or technical issues via chat, email, and phone, particularly for escalated or high\-impact cases. * Become a product expert, developing a deep understanding of how Heidi works and how customers use it in real\-world clinical contexts. * Provide exceptional technical support by clearly understanding customer needs, communicating effectively, and resolving issues accurately and efficiently. * Perform in\-depth analysis and debugging of complex technical issues, applying sound judgment and care in high\-trust healthcare environments. * Own customer issues from initial contact through to resolution, ensuring timely, accurate responses within agreed service levels. * Collaborate closely with engineering and product teams to understand, reproduce, and resolve complex technical problems \- ensuring escalations are clearly documented well\-scoped, and aligned with customer impact. * Monitor server health, system status, and bug reports, taking immediate action to mitigate impact, escalate critical issues, and prevent recurring problems. * Proactively identify systemic or recurring issues and contribute to improvements across tooling, processes, and systems that increase product reliability, support efficiency, and the team’s ability to scale, including helping identify and address recurring issues. **Who You Are:** * 2\+ years’ experience in a support engineer role. * A proactive individual who thrives in a fast\-paced startup environment and enjoys collaborating with an international team dedicated to building impactful AI experiences. * Strong problem\-solving skills with the ability to think critically and learn on the fly. * Strong customer focus \- you excel at and genuinely enjoy helping customers. * Excellent communication skills, including the ability to question, clarify, empathise, use appropriate tone and the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical users in a clear, empathetic manner. * Understanding of web development, with some experience using Python, React, Node, FastAPI, and MongoDB. **What do we believe in?** Heidi builds for the future of healthcare, not just the next quarter, and our goals are ambitious because the world’s health demands it. We believe in progress built through precision, pace, and ownership. * Live Forever \- Every release moves care forward: measured, safe, and built to last. Data guides us, but patients define the truth that matters. * Practice Ownership \- Decisions follow logic and proof, not hierarchy. Exceptional care demands exceptional standards in our work, our thinking, and our character. * Small Cuts Heal Faster \- Stability earns trust, speed delivers impact. Progress is about learning fast without breaking what people depend on. * Make others better \- Feedback is direct, kindness is constant, and excellence lifts everyone. Our success is measured by collective growth, not individual output. Our mission is clear: expand the world’s capacity to care, and do it without losing the humanity that makes care worth delivering. **Why join Heidi Health?** * Real product momentum. We're not trying to generate interest, we're channeling it. * Equity from day one. When Heidi wins, you win. You'll share directly in the success you help create. * Unmatched impact. Play a pivotal role at a critical growth moment \- working on a product that delivers tangible, real\-world value to clinicians and patients every day. * Work alongside world\-class talent. Join a team of operators and builders who've scaled unicorns. * Your health, covered. Comprehensive private medical and dental cover through Bupa, plus 24/7 mental health, coaching and wellbeing support through Sonder and a £100/month Healthy Heidi’s stipend. * Global parental leave. 26 weeks paid for primary carers and 18 weeks for secondary carers, subject to eligibility. * Fertility support. £7,000 one\-off payment, eligibility applies. * Learning \& development. £700 per year for courses, books, memberships, conferences and more. * Home office budget. £500 one\-off to set up a workspace you actually want to work in. * Recharge days after major milestones and busy periods so you can reset and come back strong. * Work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year, wherever the world takes you. * Clinical leave. 10 days per year for eligible clinical roles to maintain accreditation and requirements. * Flexibility that works. A hybrid environment, with 3 days in the office. **Heidi’s commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion** Heidi is dedicated to creating an equitable, inclusive, and supportive work environment that brings people together from diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. Our strength is in our differences. We're proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are proud to welcome all applicants as we're committed to promoting a culture of opportunity for all.

title: Data Analyst / Power BI Developer
description: **Job Type:** Data Analyst / Power BI Developer **Duration:** 6\-Month Daily Rate (with potential to extend) **Job Description:** Our client, a leading financial services organisation, is seeking an experienced Data Analyst to support the enhancement and delivery of enterprise reporting and analytics solutions across a modern cloud\-based data platform. This role will focus on data analysis, reporting development, testing, and business engagement within a fast\-paced environment. **Job Responsibilities** * Gather and interpret business requirements, translating them into clear reporting and data specifications. * Analyse, validate and reconcile datasets within a cloud\-based data warehouse environment using SQL. * Develop and maintain interactive Power BI dashboards and reports to support business decision\-making. * Support the enhancement of data models, semantic layers and reporting calculations to improve self\-service analytics capabilities. * Participate in system integration testing and user acceptance testing activities, including defect identification and resolution support. * Investigate and resolve day\-to\-day data and reporting issues in collaboration with technical and business teams. * Contribute to data governance and data quality initiatives by supporting controls, reconciliations and validation processes. * Produce and maintain documentation including report specifications, testing evidence and user support materials. * Engage with key stakeholders across business and technical teams to ensure reporting solutions align with operational requirements. * Assist in driving data adoption and continuous improvement initiatives across the organisation. **Experience Required** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a Data Analyst, BI Analyst or Reporting Analyst position. * Strong SQL experience with the ability to analyse and validate complex datasets. * Proven hands\-on experience developing reports and dashboards using Power BI. * Experience working with cloud\-based data platforms such as Snowflake or similar technologies. * Strong understanding of data modelling concepts and reporting best practices. * Experience supporting testing activities including UAT and system testing. * Ability to troubleshoot reporting and data\-related issues in a business\-critical environment. * Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Experience operating within regulated or data\-intensive environments would be advantageous. * Excellent analytical, problem\-solving and organisational skills. **Desirable Skills** * Experience with DAX calculations and semantic modelling within Power BI. * Exposure to data governance and data quality frameworks. * Understanding of cloud\-based analytics and reporting ecosystems. * Familiarity with Agile delivery methodologies. * Previous experience supporting self\-service reporting environments. * Financial services or other highly regulated industry experience is beneficial. **Educational Requirements** * Third\-level degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Data Analytics, Business Information Systems or a related discipline. **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role, please apply for this role with your updated CV

title: Data Center Technician - Technical IT 1 (contract)
description: Please note that this is a contract role providing services to Microsoft through external staffing partners of Allegis Global Solutions (AGS). If you are selected for this role, you will be employed by AGS and will not be an employee of Microsoft. **Role Overview** Data Centre Technician – Technical IT is an entry‑level, hands‑on data centre role focused on decommissioning hardware, cable removal, and basic physical IT tasks. The role follows clear procedures and checklists with guidance from senior technicians. This is not a scanning or advanced engineering role \- it is physical, process‑driven work inside a data centre. **Roles \& Responsibilities** * Remove network and power cables during data centre decommissioning * Run, label, stage, and organize power and network cabling * Perform DBD removals only (no scanning or data processing) * Prepare, stage, start up, and shut down hardware using written guides * Always follow standard operating procedures and safety rules * Assist with installing, replacing, and decommissioning IT equipment * Conduct physical audits of data centre assets * Work on‑site during core business hours; flexibility for extended hours if needed **Must‑Have Skills** * High school diploma / GED / equivalent * Basic understanding of: + Computer hardware + Servers and IT components * Ability to follow written instructions, checklists, and procedures * Working knowledge of: + Microsoft Outlook + Microsoft Excel + Microsoft Word * Comfortable with physical, hands‑on work in a data centre environment **Nice‑to‑Have Skills** * Prior data centre or IT hardware support experience * Experience with cable management or rack/stack work * Familiarity with asset tagging or physical inventory audits * Exposure to decommissioning or migration projects **Experience Level Guidance** * 0–2 years’ experience * Suitable for: + Entry‑level IT technicians + Field support technicians + Hardware or datacenter support associates * Not a senior or design role **Additional Details** * Location: Grange castle, Ireland (ONSITE) * Duration: 3 months * Shift Timings: Monday – Friday 8am \- 4:30pm (should be flexible work hours) * Pay Range: €22\- €26/hr (PAYE) * Weekly Schedule: 40 hours As a workplace, Allegis Global Solutions focuses on relationships – with each other, our clients and our candidates. In fact, serving others is one of our core values. We support open communication and recognize that giving constructive criticism can be even harder than receiving it. We appreciate the fearless and the passionate, who force us to be better. Everything we do sits on a pillar of diversity, equity and inclusion \- diverse perspectives, backgrounds and ideas achieve innovation and make us successful. Please note that we may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to screen, assess, or select applicants for this position. These tools may analyse application materials and assist our team in identifying candidates whose qualifications best match the requirements of the role. If you have questions about our use of AI in the hiring process, or would like more information, please contact us. **Pay Rate Range** 22 \- 26 EUR hourly

title: Data Scientist - Underwriting Analytics Dublin/Wexford
description: **Job Summary** Zurich Insurance Company Europe AG Ireland branch is looking for a Data Scientist – Underwriting Analytics within the Actuarial Pricing function. This role is ideal for an analytically strong, hands on data professional with solid coding skills and experience in statistical modelling, machine learning and/or AI, who wants to apply these capabilities to real insurance underwriting and pricing challenges. You will work across both Personal Lines and Commercial Lines, leading complex analyses, developing models and tools, and turning data into actionable insights. You will also help to mentor and support junior team members in their development. This role may be available part\-time or full time. This is because we want the best people for our roles, and we recognise that sometimes those people aren’t available full\-time. Please talk to us at interview about the flexibility you may need. **Your Role** As a Data Scientist your main responsibilities will include, but not necessarily be limited to, the following: * Leading the development of analytical solutions and models to support underwriting and pricing decisions. * Designing, building and maintaining robust data pipelines, datasets and codebases that support regular MI, dashboards and advanced analytics. * Taking ownership of more complex reporting and forecasting processes, ensuring they are accurate, scalable and well\-documented. * Enhancing existing MI for Underwriting and Performance KPIs, introducing more advanced analytics. * Developing and maintaining financial and performance forecasting models to support planning and portfolio steering. **Your Skills \& Experience** As a Data Scientist your skills and qualifications will include: * Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent) in Mathematics, Statistics, Actuarial Science, Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, Finance or a related quantitative discipline. * Typically 2–5 years of experience in data science, advanced analytics, actuarial, or a closely related field. * Strong coding skills in at least one of Python or R, with experience of writing clean, reusable, well\-structured code. * Practical experience with statistical modelling and/or machine learning techniques, such as: * Generalised linear models (GLMs) or other regression methods * Tree\-based methods (e.g. random forests, gradient boosting) * Time series modelling and forecasting * Clustering and segmentation technique * Experience with data visualisation tools such as Power BI or Tableau (designing and publishing dashboards for business users). **Additional Information** Primary work location is Wexford/Dublin but occasional travel may be required to other Zurich locations. If you value an exciting and varied working environment and meet the above requirements, then our recruiter is looking forward to receiving your application by clicking on the button ***“Apply online”.*** **Who We Are** Zurich is one of Ireland's leading insurance companies providing a wide range of general insurance and life insurance products and services. The company employs over 1,000 people across its locations in Dublin and Wexford. Zurich is a strong brand – with our 53,000 employees worldwide form the basis of our success, helping our customers in 210 countries and territories to understand and protect themselves from risk. In order to deliver our services, we offer our employees flexible working models and interesting opportunities for further training \& development. As a Zurich employee you benefit from a multitude of advantages as well as a strong culture, characterized by acceptance, diversity and team spirit. At Zurich, we foster a culture of diversity and inclusion. Our purpose and values are designed to protect, inspire confidence and help our employees reach their full potential. We value and defend what is right and promote opportunities for equity among our professionals, regardless of gender, disability, LGBTQ \+, race, ethnicity, generations, belief, etc. Join Zurich and be part of this culture. Together we are committed to delivering on our purpose \- Let’s Create a Brighter Future Together!

title: Day Shift Security Officer
description: 📍 **Dublin 9** 🕒 **Fully Flexible, Monday to Sunday, minimum 36 hours per week** 💰 **€15\.41 per hour plus €20\.00 night allowance and Sunday premium** Join Today and Build a Better Tomorrow! At Securitas Security Services we are united by our purpose of helping make your world a safer place, we are committed to ethics, sustainability, and compliance, our core values – Integrity, Vigilance and Helpfulness .We have created a culture where our people are empowered to be the best they can be. We believe in celebrating our differences and creating a supportive environment where all voices are heard. Our colleagues are empowered to think big and push the boundaries of innovation. As a purpose\-led organisation, we aspire to make a difference every day \- and we’re looking for the best and brightest to join our team. Career opportunities to fuel your future, together we can invest in your professional and personal growth. No matter where you are in your career, there are many training and development opportunities. Your career is in your hands, and we will help you maximize your potential! We are always looking to grow too! Join our team and use your talent and passion to help build a safer, healthier, and more efficient world. **Job Description** **Securitas Security Services are currently recruiting Customer Service Security Officers for day shifts only to be based in our large retail client site in Dublin 9\.** **Rate of pay is €15\.41 per hour plus €3\.44 on Sunday and night shift allowance of €20\.00** * Free onsite parking and subsidized canteen facilities and easily accessible by public transport\* **Main Purpose Of Position** To protect our customer’s property, people and/or assets by providing security services to a specific site in direct accordance with the sites published Assignment Instructions (AI’s) and any subsequent changes. **Expectations Of This Position** * Fully and satisfactorily complete all of the training that has been agreed is necessary for the particular site. * Fully and satisfactorily complete all of the tasks and duties on site as defined within the published Assignment Instructions (AI’s). * Proactively refer to and review the published Assignment Instructions, thus familiarising oneself with any changes and in order to suggest changes that can proactively improve or enhance the level of service. * Look out for, and to immediately report, any Health \& Safety hazards, or potential hazards, on site. * Stay alert to any threats or changes within the work environment in order to take the appropriate action in order to reduce the chance of disruption, loss or damage. * Use common sense and initiative by immediately involving others if a situation appears to become threatening. * Complete an accurate, detailed notebook entry as soon as possible after any incident, which can then be used as part of the Incident Report process. * Wear the uniform provided correctly at all times, ensuring all items is clean and pressed. To maintain an excellent level of personal grooming and hygiene at all times. * Maintain a helpful, interested and courteous approach to team members, customer’s staff and the general public, as applicable in order to uphold a highly professional image. **Qualifications** **Security Officer Specification:** * Good English essential * Excellent written and attention to detail skills are essential * Good level of P.C. skills * Previous experience desirable, however training will be provided * Must have excellent communication skills * Be flexible with your working hours. * PSA Licence * Strong Customer Service experience desirable **Additional Information** At Securitas, we believe in rewarding our people for their hard work. We offer a range of great benefits designed to support our people and their families. These comprehensive benefits include discounted health care scheme, company pension, bike to work scheme, access to hundreds of discounts from well\-known brands and retailers, access to hundreds of high\-quality online education courses from some of the world’s leading learning institutions, ways to save for the future, and other resources to improve health and well\-being. **Benefits** * Full\-time 36 hours a week contract of employment with the ability to work up to 48 hours per week * 24 hour EAP (Employee Assistance Programme) free confidential counselling and advice supporting our employees in a number of areas. * Employer Contribution Pension Scheme from the day you join the Company * Progression, training and development opportunities * Christmas Savings Scheme * Discounted Private Healthcare Scheme (Cash back on everyday medical costs, such as dental check\-ups, optical, GP and Emergency Department visits.) * Internal recognition schemes * Bicycle to work scheme from the day you join the Company (Save up to 52% on a bike and accessories) * Employee Refer a friend incentives * Life Assurance equal to one year’s basic pay as per Employment Regulation Order (ERO) * Overtime as per the ERO * Sick Pay Scheme as per ERO * SIPTU Representation Rights * Substantial Maternity Leave top up payment ***Securitas is an equal opportunities Employer*** **P.S.A. Licence Number : 00352** **Website:** https://www.securitas.ie/ **Available jobs:** https://www.securitas.ie/careers/available\-jobs/ **Benefits at Securitas:** https://www.securitas.ie/careers/benefits\-at\-securitas/ **Securitas Integrity Line:** https://www.securitas.ie/sustainability/securitas\-integrity\-line/

title: Deputy CSR
description: **Job Description** **Job Title:** **Deputy CSR** **Department: Radiology** **Reports to: Head of Radiology Operations or assigned Delegate** **Date: 2026** **Overall Purpose of Job** We will provide exceptional patient care in an environment where quality, respect, caring and compassion are at the centre of all we do. While carrying out the duties appropriate to a Radiographer in the department, maintenance of professional competence in other areas of the department is necessary as some participation in the on\-call service is required. This will necessitate the post holder in rotating through other areas in the department on a periodic basis. **Key Responsibilities and Deliverables** Supporting the CSR of the modality to run the service, including covering in their absence and ensuring standards of care are maintained. To undertake modality specific examinations as required and to a high standard. To take part in on\-call service as required. Radiation safety: To ensure that the three principles of radiation protection, namely justification, optimisation (ALARA) and dose constraints/limits are adhered to. To be familiar with EU Council Directive Euratom Basic safety standards as set out in current Irish legislation for the protection of health workers and the general public against the dangers arising from Ionising Radiation (S.I. 30 of 2019\) and the health protection of individuals against the dangers of ionising radiation in relation to medical exposure (S.I. 256 of 2018\). To take part in routine daily inspection of equipment and quality assurance procedures. To report any defects or problems either directly to the Medical Physics and Clinical Specialist in the area where working. To be aware of own limitations in terms of expertise and experience and not to be afraid to ask for assistance or advice from more senior staff. To follow directions of Head of Radiology Operations, Consultant Radiologist, Clinical Specialist, more experienced staff and departmental protocols where applicable. To keep up\-to\-date with current imaging techniques and developments in the required modality and to take part in in\-service training as required. To actively participate in Continued Professional Development. To take responsibility for the general cleanliness, tidiness and safety of the Department, with specific responsibility for their own work area. To familiarise themselves with current infection control procedures. To be familiar with, and abide by, all Health and Safety policies and recommendations. To provide a quality service for patients by ensuring that every patient is treated as an individual in terms of courtesy, kindness, efficiency, efficacy and confidentiality. To participate and support research projects and undertake training of more junior or less experienced staff. To carry out any other duties as directed by the Head of Radiology Operations or Clinical Specialist in area where working. To maintain good working relationships with colleagues and with non\-radiographic staff, both in the Radiology Department and elsewhere in the hospital. Other duties assigned that are appropriate to the post To use one's initiative in a safe and effective manner as set out in the Radiation Safety Procedures to the benefit of the patient All posts in Radiology Services will be continually assessed in line with service requirements and changes with the development of the Hospital. Consequently, the duties of the post may change. **Quality:** Assist the Head of Radiology Operations with Policies and Procedure development as appropriate Implement quality improvement as necessary to ensure safety of patients and staff Perform departmental audits that feed into the organisation quality programme Attend relevant meetings as requested by Head of Radiology Assist Head of Radiology in the preparation for accreditation surveys **Person Specification** **Qualifications** BSc (Hons) Diagnostic Radiography Qualification must be recognised and validated by the Irish Institute of Radiography. CORU Registered **Experience** Minimum of 6 years post qualification experience in in the chosen modality or a post graduate qualification in the modality plus a minimum of 3 years' experience. Demonstrate experience of working with a team of Radiographers. **Job Specific Competencies and Knowledge** Demonstrate ability to manage clinical and non\-clinical situations. Have proven ability to work under pressure. Have excellent interpersonal and communication skills. Demonstrate evidence of continuing professional development. Have a high capacity for responsibility and individual initiative. Be able to deal with staff and management at all levels within the organisation. **Personal Competencies** All posts in Beacon Hospital require a high level of flexibility to ensure the delivery of an effective and efficient service. Therefore, the post holder will be required to demonstrate flexibility as and when required by their manager or hospital management. **This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the Hospital and the post holder develop, this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the Hospital.**

title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: Device Engineering Systems Intern
description: McDermott Laboratories Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. Our Global Device Development organisation is focused on the design, development and commercialisation of a diverse portfolio of device technology solutions to enable delivery for our large and growing generic and branded products around the globe. We develop device technologies to enable drug\-delivery for respiratory, biologic, biosimilar and injectable products, and work across Viatris to support innovative technology solutions to provide healthcare solutions for our patients. We operate from 4 sites: Dublin, Ireland, Cambridge \& Sandwich, UK and Southpointe, Pittsburgh, USA. Every member of our team is dedicated to creating better health for a better world, one person at a time. **The Role \& What You Will Be Doing** Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the Device Supplier Assurance Intern role will make an impact: * Support the generation of detailed purchasing specification, aligning to product and process requirements. * Collaborate with key stakeholders to gather information and insights for accurate generation of Purchase Specification * Create and maintain detailed timelines to achieve planned program deliverables * Assist in the support and completion of defined documentation deliverables * Generate defect reports and associated QMS system administrative actions for fulfilment of deliverables against key program milestones to support wider GDD \& DPT actions * Engage with functional line team to communicate escalations and key program objectives pertaining to Packaging and Labelling. * Attend where necessary both functional line and department related meetings **About Your Skills \& Experience** For this role, we’re looking for a candidate who has an effective combination of the following qualifications, skills and experiences: * Currently enrolled in a degree program in Biomedical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Life Sciences, or a related technical field. * Strong interest in medical technology, device development and manufacturing processes. * Good organizational skills and attention to detail. * Strong communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team. * Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, Outlook). At Viatris, we are dedicated to building a truly diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t fully align with every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles. **Benefits At Viatris** At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Some of our benefits include the following on top of a competitive salary: * Excellent career progression opportunities * Work\-life balance initiatives **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Dispatch Operative
description: **Dispatch Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Dispatch Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK food dispatch activity. This role is a highly focused on food dispatching. A Dispatch Operative is responsible for the accurate, efficient, and safe preparation and loading of food products for customer delivery. Working in the final stage of the production line, this role ensures products are correctly packed to the store's crates, working efficiently in fast\-paced in the chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Picking customer orders accurately, assembling, and preparing finished goods for transport in line with production schedules. * Safely preparation, loading and unloading delivery crates. Labelling crates, ensuring they meet quality specifications and are properly organized. * Monitoring product quality, ensuring no damaged or incorrect goods are dispatched. * Adhering to strict Food Safety, HACCP, and Health \& Safety regulations, including keeping the dispatch area clean and tidy and organize. * Adhere to hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes, jackets, hats \& gloves). * Completing all necessary paperwork, such as delivery dockets and stock levels checks for stock movements and traceability. * Follow stock rotation and FIFO (First\-In, First\-Out) to ensures perishable goods are dispatched before expiration. * Move packed goods to shelves, racks, crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Good numeracy and literacy skills for checking orders, stock rotation, and documentation. * High attention to detail, ability to work under pressure to meet tight deadlines, and strong team\-working abilities. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. Ability to handle repetitive, manual lifting of items in chilled/cold environments. Availability to work late evening shift (2pm \- 11pm) Monday \- Saturday.
